title: Canopy and cavern content depth phase
summary: Forty-fifth structured packet for Eco Explorer. This lane-2 wave supports the newer forest vertical spaces with one canopy-and-cavern content pack and then one compact memory payoff through existing journal, close-look, comparison, or sketchbook seams.

execution_order.why_now: The newer forest vertical spaces are strong enough to deserve more authored natural history.
A few strong vertical-space discoveries will do more than a broad content swell.
The pack needs to stay science-safe, calm, and lane-owned.

execution_order.goal: Prepare one compact memory payoff for the new vertical spaces.
execution_order.why_now: Once the content lands, the best follow-on is one quiet payoff that makes those spaces more memorable.
The vertical spaces should feel not just full, but worth remembering.
The payoff should stay compact and lane-owned.

guardrails: Reuse existing journal, comparison, close-look, or sketchbook surfaces.
Prefer a few strong forest vertical anchors over a wider taxonomy wave.
Do not reopen route, station, or traversal runtime.
Favor one canopy habitat carrier plus one seep-surface habitat carrier over another cave-fauna branch.
Keep the pack to two new entries and two compact ecosystem notes.
acceptance_focus: The crown-rest, bark-window, and cave-pocket spaces feel more inhabited.
One compact memory payoff makes those spaces more memorable.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/lane-runner.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, .agents/lanes/lane-2.md, docs/reports/2026-03-31-canopy-and-cavern-content-depth-phase.md, docs/reports/2026-03-31-canopy-and-cavern-content-pack-handoff.md, and this packet. Treat this as lane 2's next content wave: use one canopy habitat carrier (`canopy-moss-bed`), one seep-surface habitat carrier (`seep-moss-mat`), and two compact notes to deepen the newer forest vertical spaces without reopening route, station, or traversal runtime.
